cards.back: A 16-bit error detection code used in computer networks to detect errors in data transmission
Using a polynomial equation (x^16 + x^12 + x^5 + 1), the input data is transformed into a 16-bit code

cards.front: What is the purpose of CRC-CCITT?
cards.back: To detect single-bit errors and some multi-bit errors in data transmission

cards.front: What type of errors can CRC-CCITT detect?
cards.back: Single-bit errors and some multi-bit errors such as burst errors and single-error burst errors
It is used in data transmission protocols such as point-to-point protocols and frame relay protocols
